summary: Russia launched a mid‑range ballistic missile "Oreshnyk" that struck the Russian‑occupied area near Avdiivka. Ukrainian forces continue to report numerous UAV movements across northern, eastern and southern fronts. Both sides experience ongoing air‑defence alerts and drone attacks.
key_points: Russia used a mid‑range ballistic missile "Oreshnyk" on territory temporarily controlled by Russia near Avdiivka (Source: @eRadarrua/93614).
Multiple UAVs were observed over northern Kyiv region heading toward the capital, including over the Kyiv water reservoir (Source: @kpszsu/62726).
Groups of Shahed drones were reported launched from Chernihiv region toward Kyiv's Vyshorod district (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67218).
Ukrainian defense minister advisor hinted at significant changes to mobilization policy (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30408).
A night‑time attack damaged the apartments of President Zelensky and businessman Timur Mindich on 9A Grushevsky Street in Kyiv (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30398).
Ukrainian troops in Mala Tokmachka, Zaporizhzhia region, celebrated a record of holding the line for over 1500 days (Source: @UkraineNow/81449).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions (Source: @kpszsu/62724).

sections.executive_summary: Russia employed a mid‑range ballistic missile "Oreshnyk" striking near Avdiivka, marking a notable escalation. Ukrainian forces reported extensive UAV activity across multiple fronts, including attacks on Kyiv and southern regions. Both sides continue to experience high‑intensity air‑defence alerts and drone strikes.

points: Ukrainian defense minister advisor Sternenko announced upcoming significant changes to mobilization, though details were not provided, indicating potential shifts in manpower policy (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30408).

Ukrainian troops in the village of Mala Tokmachka, Zaporizhzhia oblast, have maintained the front line for over 1500 days, earning a record in the Book of Records of Ukraine (Source: @UkraineNow/81449).
A nighttime missile attack damaged the apartments of President Zelensky and businessman Timur Mindich at 9A Grushevsky Street in Kyiv (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30398).
Ukrainian MP Aleksey Goncharenko called for an end to the war following the overnight attack on Kyiv, emphasizing civilian suffering (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30395).
Russia launched a mid‑range ballistic missile "Oreshnyk" that struck the temporarily Russian‑controlled area near Avdiivka (Source: @eRadarrua/93614).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions, indicating intensified air‑strike operations (Source: @kpszsu/62724).
Russia appointed Vitaly Shulika as Deputy Defense Minister, suggesting possible changes in defence leadership (Source: @tass_agency/377464).
Russian officials reiterated the country's openness to constructive global economic interaction, reflecting diplomatic messaging amid conflict (Source: @tass_agency/377436).
UAV groups were observed moving from Kharkiv region toward Izium and Balaklia on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/62738).
Shahed drone launches from Chernihiv region were reported heading toward Kyiv's Vyshorod district, affecting eastern approaches to the capital (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67218).
Ukrainian reconnaissance UAVs were spotted over the Black Sea area near Sumy and Kharkiv, indicating surveillance activity on the eastern maritime flank (Source: @eRadarrua/93613).
A civilian in Kharkiv assisted TCC employees in mobilizing a young man who refused to show documents, reflecting local security dynamics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30400).
UAVs were observed over northern Kyiv region, flying over the Kyiv water reservoir toward the capital (Source: @kpszsu/62726).
Multiple UAV groups were reported in Chernihiv region heading southwest, south, and toward Nizhyn, indicating active aerial reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/62710, @kpszsu/62717, @kpszsu/62711).
Shahed drone launches from Chernihiv region targeted Kyiv's Vyshorod district, heightening northern air threat (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67218).
UAV activity was noted near the city of Obukhiv from the north, suggesting potential threats to Kyiv's southern approaches (Source: @kpszsu/62729).
Ukrainian forces reported a FAB (cluster bomb) strike on a multi‑storey building in Druzhkivka, Donetsk, causing civilian casualties including a 70‑year‑old woman (Source: @UkraineNow/81440).
Guided aerial bombs were launched by Russian tactical aviation on Zaporizhzhia oblast, indicating intensified southern offensive air operations (Source: @kpszsu/62724).
UAV sightings over Mykolaiv region included locations Luperove, Snihurivka, and a polygon, showing continued aerial activity in the south (Source: @eRadarrua/93575).
A missile strike in the Sumy region was reported as a reactive system moving toward Ladan, reflecting cross‑regional missile deployments (Source: @eRadarrua/93618).
Air‑raid alert sounded in Kyiv at 10:50 on 25 May 2026, indicating ongoing aerial threat (Source: @eRadarrua/93595).
Shahed drone launches from Chernihiv region targeted Kyiv's Vyshorod district, part of a broader swarm of unmanned attacks (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67218).
Russian tactical aviation employed guided aerial bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk, showing use of precision strike capabilities (Source: @kpszsu/62724).
Ukraine reported a GPS jamming incident affecting the British Defence Minister's aircraft near the Russian border, highlighting electronic warfare threats (Source: @UkraineNow/81442).

key_developments: Russia launched a mid‑range ballistic missile "Oreshnyk" that struck the temporarily Russian‑controlled area near Avdiivka (Source: @eRadarrua/93614).
Multiple Shahed drones were launched from Chernihiv region toward Kyiv's Vyshorod district, increasing the northern aerial threat (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67218).
Ukrainian forces in Mala Tokmachka, Zaporizhzhia, have held the line for over 1500 days, marking a significant defensive achievement (Source: @UkraineNow/81449).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs on Zaporizhzhia and Donetsk regions, indicating intensified air‑strike operations (Source: @kpszsu/62724).
Ukrainian defense minister advisor Sternenko announced upcoming major changes to mobilization policy (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30408).
A night‑time missile attack damaged the apartments of President Zelensky and businessman Timur Mindich on 9A Grushevsky Street in Kyiv (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30398).
Air‑raid alert sounded in Kyiv at 10:50 on 25 May 2026, reflecting continued air‑defence alerts (Source: @eRadarrua/93595).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian missile use and guided bomb deployments demonstrate a shift toward higher‑precision, longer‑range strikes, especially near Avdiivka and southern fronts. Ukrainian UAV activity across northern and eastern sectors indicates sustained reconnaissance and potential strike capabilities, while Shahed drone swarms pose a persistent low‑altitude threat to Kyiv. Electronic warfare, exemplified by GPS jamming of a British aircraft, adds a complex layer to the air conflict, challenging both sides' situational awareness.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine continues to reinforce defensive positions, highlighted by the record‑holding defense in Mala Tokmachka and ongoing mobilization reforms. International actors, such as the European Commission, have pledged additional air‑defence assistance, reflecting heightened support amid escalating aerial threats. Russian leadership changes and diplomatic statements suggest an intent to sustain military pressure while managing domestic and international perceptions.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV trajectories in the north and east remains critical for early warning of potential strikes on key infrastructure. The deployment of the "Oreshnyk" missile indicates Russia's capability to field mid‑range ballistic systems, requiring updated threat models. Shahed drone launch patterns from Chernihiv warrant intensified air‑defence readiness around Kyiv. Continued observation of electronic warfare incidents is essential to protect command and control assets.
